analysis.consistency: The data appears internally consistent with typical D&D 5e spell structures. The components (V, S), casting time (reaction), duration (1 round), and effects (+5 AC, Magic Missile immunity) are standard for the Shield spell.
analysis.quality: High. The data is clean, well-structured, and complete for a spell entry. The use of cross-references (e.g., `[_magic missile_]`, `[invisible]`) suggests proper linking in the source system.

mechanics.name: Shield
mechanics.trigger: When hit by an attack or targeted by Magic Missile.
mechanics.effect: Grants +5 AC against the triggering attack and all attacks until the start of your next turn. Grants immunity to damage from Magic Missile for the duration.
mechanics.assessment: Mechanically sound. This is a highly effective defensive reaction spell. The dual benefit (AC boost against all subsequent attacks this round, plus specific Magic Missile immunity) is clearly defined.

analysis.potential_issues: None observed in the provided raw data structure. The cross-references are present, which is good, but their validity depends on the linked entities existing.
